film.title: Amman Kovil Kizhakale
film.overview: Kanmani, a wealthy girl, decides to teach Chinnamani a lesson by pretending to be in love with him. Things, however, take an ugly turn when Chinnamani marries her in a fit of rage.

dialogues.dialogue: You? - No, sir. We were talking amongst ourselves. Sorry! Stop! Shame on such a marriage. Your parents did not even give me a bag. They have deceived us. Don't look there! Look down.

'Today's age.' 'And from a daughter-in-law I became a mother-in-law.' It's a shame to this marriage! Munni! - It's a shame to this marriage! Your father had said that he would give fifty thousand as dowry.

'And from a daughter-in-law I became a mother-in-law.' It's a shame to this marriage! Munni! - It's a shame to this marriage! Your father had said that he would give fifty thousand as dowry. But he did not give a single penny.
Like you I will not suppress my feelings. What did you say? You shameless girl! You... you... you have... you have...accused Suraj? That means you have accused me.
Kamli, lift up her veil. Move back! Sister, don't be so proud of your wealth. I came here with my daughter-in-law to give your respect....and not to degrade ourselves. Take back your gift.

Father, don't be worried. Your son is going to die for the sake of our country's freedom. You should be proud of me. Yes, my son. I am proud! I am very proud. But after you are hanged will these people allow us to take you away....home for the last rites?

Your son is going to die for the sake of our country's freedom. You should be proud of me. Yes, my son. I am proud! I am very proud. But after you are hanged will these people allow us to take you away....home for the last rites? No, father. Why is that necessary?
